fertilizer_Recommendation:
The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Capsicum: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are about 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) and later about 4 weeks with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost and 50 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 50 gm/plant of CAN. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil content of zinc and organic matter should be improved. 
Tomato: Prior to transplanting apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 80 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial. 
Capsicum: During the land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure/ compost. At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing plants with a foliar feed. 
Okra: At planting time apply  in bands on the side furrow 6 tons/acre of manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with CAN in 2 equal split applications. The first top dress should be done 3-4 weeks after planting at 35 kg/acre and the second application 3-4 weeks later at the same rate.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and magnesium are deficient. Soil content of zinc and organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH and magnesium content liming with 200 kg/acre of dolomite is recommended at least 3 weeks before planting. 
Chilli: During the land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing plants with a foliar feed. 
Melons: At planting time apply 5 tons/acre of manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants start to "run" top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. When plants about to flower apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Maize: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top-dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil content of zinc and organic matter should be improved. 
Cabbage: At transplanting time use 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Also apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 40 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later. 
Capsicum: During the land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure/ compost. At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing plants with a foliar feed.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Soil fertility is poor. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Vegetables: At transplanting time use 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost per planting hole (over 4 tons/acre). Also apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 20-25 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later. 
Maize: Apply 4 tons/acre of manure or compost during the land preparation. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high, top-dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
French beans: Before planting apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN at 2-3 leave stage and 50 kg/acre at the onset of flowering. An application of foliar feed such as bayfolan is recommended from the fourth week after planting to mid-podding stage.

The soil reaction is strongly(1,2) acid to moderately acid (3,4). Nirtogen (1,2,3,4), Phosphorus(1,2,3,4), Organic carbon(1,4), Potassium(1,2,3,4), Calcium(1), Magnesium(1), Zinc(1), and Manganese(1) are deficiecient,. Liming is recommended for Farm 1 and 2 at the rate of 400kg/acre using dolomite or Koru Lime. Broadcast the lime after ploughing at leats 2 months before planting.  At planting place three handfull of well decomposed manure/compost into planting holes and mix with the soil. Apply 150kg/acre of N:P:K (17:17:17) fertilizer. Top dress with 150kg/acre using CAN.

The soil reaction is very strogly acid(8), strongly acid(5&6) to moderate acid(7). Nitrogen, Phosphorus and Zinc(8) are deficient. Liming is strongly recommended(5,6,8) at the rate of 800kg/acre of dolomitic/ Koru Lime. Broadcast lime after ploughing at least 2 months before planting. At planting place three handfull of well decomposed manure/compost into planting holes and mix with the soil. Apply 150kg/acre of N:P:K (17:17:17) fertilizer. Top dress with 150kg/acre using CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Cabbage: Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Kale: Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3 weeks later. 
Tomato: At the start of flowering top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

All fields have similar soil fertility condition. The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Soil phosphorus and zinc are deficient in Upper 318 field. The soil content of organic matter is sufficient. Broadcast on the ground 50 gm per bush of diammonium phosphate (DAP). Manure should be applied one debe (10 kg) per bush per year. Deficiency of zinc is corrected by foliar sprays of zinc sulphate.

The soil pH is satisfactory for plants' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Banana: At transplanting time apply a bucket of 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm of CAN per stool in year one and thereafter 100 gm of CAN per year at the beginning of rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers, the soil should be tested annually. 
Coffee: At transplanting time apply a bucket of 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of CAN and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm/tree of CAN in 3-4 equal applications per year. All nutrient material should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy.  To determine the right amount of fertilizers, the soil should be tested annually. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 50 kg/acre of CAN. Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN.

All fields have similar soil fertility condition. The soil is very alkaline for plants' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. High values of electrical conductivity and sodium suggest the possibility of saline-sodic condition. The soil alkalinity may be reduced by applying 400 kg/acre of elemental sulphur. The addition of sulphur is usually accompanied by irrigation to get water into the soil and, with good drainage, to carry off the excess and to remove unwanted products of the reaction between the sulphur and the soil. The soil treatment should be done 3- 4 weeks before planting. 
Tomato: During the land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 150 kg/acre of diammonium phosphate (DAP). Top dress with 50 kg/acre of ammonium sulphate (AS) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of AS at the start of flowering is beneficial. 
Maize: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of manure/compost. At planting time apply 150 kg/acre of DAP. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of AS. 
Green grams: At planting apply 4 tons/acre of manure/compost and 150 kg/acre of DAP.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Tomato: Prior to transplanting apply 4 kg per square metre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Topdress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN four weeks later is beneficial. 
Passion fruit: Mix the topsoil with a debe (10 kg) of well decomposed manure or compost, 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra topsoil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plant's growth apply 50 gm/plant of CAN one month after planting.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil potassium and organic matter should be improved. 
Tomato: In order to improve zinc and organic matter of the soil apply 4 kg/m2 of well decomposed manure or compost prior to transplanting. When transplanting, apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. The fertilizer should be mixed well with the soil. Top-dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN four weeks later is beneficial. 
Capsicum: Apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. When plants are 15 cm high apply 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Potassium is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Maize: To raise soil pH and calcium content apply  through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime 3-4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

The soil reaction (pH) is very acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 800 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Maize: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Fruit trees: At transplanting time apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of SSP and top soil per planting hole. Top dress 50 gm per tree of CAN in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed, e.g. bayfolan. To determine the right amount of fertilizers, the soil should be tested annually. 
Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 gm of SSP per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 50 gm/plant of CAN. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is too alkaline for crops' growth.  Soil organic matter is sufficient. 
Fruit trees: The soil potassium content should be improved. At the beginning of rains apply 100 gm/ tree of sulphate of potash. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. Manure should be applied one bucket of 10 kg per tree per year. 
Baby corn: At planting time apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Top dress with ammonium sulphate (AS) at 80 kg/acre in 2 equal split applications at knee height after 1 month and when plants are about to flower. 
Peas: During the land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost.

Maize: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Potato: At planting time apply in the ridge 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. The manure and fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with potato seeds. 
Beans: Apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 along the ridges before planting. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds (legumes) to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. High value of sodium suggests the possibility of sodic condition. Soil organic matter is sufficient. 
Tomato: Prior to transplanting apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of ammonium sulphate (AS) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of AS at the start of flowering is beneficial. 
Capsicum/ Chilli: During the land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of AS. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of AS. 
Cabbage: At planting time use 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of AS when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Maize: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting apply 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) and 50 kg/acre of super phosphate (SSP). When maize is about a knee high, top-dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Wheat: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply by broadcasting 50 kg/acre of CAN and 50 kg/acre of SSP. Rotation of 3 years wheat followed by 3 years of grass helps to maintain the soil structure and greatly reduces disease incidence. Wheat is insensitive to zinc deficiency. 
Vegetables: At transplanting time use 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost per planting hole (over 4 tons/acre). Also apply 80 kg/acre of CAN and 50 kg/acre of SSP. Top-dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed.

All fields have similar soil fertility condition. The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Phosphorus is deficient in Green house 2. Soil organic matter should be improved.

Kale: During transplanting apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of SSP. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 100 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure/compost and 100 kg/acre of SSP. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for flowers' growth. Acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided due to their acidic reaction. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter should be improved. Recommendations: During the bed preparation apply 2 kg/m2 of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 gm/m2 of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dressing should be done at an interval of two months using 50 gm/ m2 of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

Soil zinc and organic matter should be improved. Recommendations: During the bed preparation apply 1 kg/m2 of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 50 gm/m2 of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Top dressing should be done at an interval of two months using 50 gm/ m2 of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen and zinc are deficient. Phosphorus is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Tomato: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of ammonium sulphate nitrate (ASN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of ASN at the start of flowering is beneficial. 
Cabbage: At planting time 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost, 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of ASN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Maize: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of ASN.

Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Maize: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. At planting time apply 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of ASN.

Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial. 
Capsicum: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting time apply 80 kg/acre of SSP. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Melons: Prior to transplanting time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and mix well with the soil. When transplanting apply 80 kg/acre of SSP. After thinning the crop is top dressed with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering.

Maize: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate  through broadcasting. At planting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 50 kg/acre of ammonium sulphate nitrate (ASN). 
Rice: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 as basic dressing. At 43-58 days after transplanting (at panicle initiation stage) apply 100 kg/acre of ASN. 
Fruit trees: At the beginning of rains apply 50 gm per tree of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. Manure or compost should be applied a bucket of 10 kg per tree per year. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Melons: At planting time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure/compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and mix well with the soil. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. After thinning the crop is top dressed with 60 kg/acre of ASN followed by 120 kg/acre of ASN prior to flowering. 
Vegetables: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of manure/compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate  through broadcasting. At planting apply 50 kg/acre of sulphate of potash. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of ASN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of ASN 3-4 weeks later.

To raise magnesium soil content liming with 300 kg/acre of dolomite is recommended at least 3 weeks before planting.

Fruit trees: At the beginning of rains apply 50 gm per tree of N:P:K 17:17:17. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. Manure or compost should be applied a bucket of 20 kg per tree per year. 
Melons: At planting time apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and mix well with the soil. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. After thinning the crop is top dressed with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Vegetables: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of manure or compost. At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later.

Fruit trees: At the beginning of rains apply 50 gm per tree of N:P:K 17:17:17. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. Melons: At planting time apply 3 tons/acre of well decomposed manure/compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and mix well with the soil. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation time apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. 
Maize: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Beans: Several days before planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. 
Vegetables: At transplanting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 100 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later.

The soil pH is alkaline for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. 
Maize: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of diammonium phosphate (DAP). When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Beans: Several days before planting apply 100 kg/acre of DAP. 
Vegetables: At transplanting time apply 100 kg/acre of DAP. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 100 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Tomato: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 4 tons/acre of manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolomite at list 3 weeks before planting. 
Tomatoes: At transplanting time apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 1 tea spoonful of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Top dress with 1 tea spoonful of CAN at early fruiting stage. An application of 40 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing plants with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Capsicum: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting time apply 40 kg/acre of CAN. When plants are about 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN and later about 4 weeks with 100 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter content should be improved. 
Potato: Just before planting apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. The manure should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with potato seeds. Apply 50 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 at planting time in furrows and mix with the soil thoroughly. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Beans: During the land preparation add 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Several days before planting apply 50 kg/acre of 23:23:0.

Potato: Just before planting apply 1-2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. The manure should be well ploughed in the soil to avoid close contact with potato seeds. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of SSP.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Wheat: At land preparation time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply by spreading 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Rotation of 3 years wheat followed by 3 years of grass helps to maintain the soil structure and greatly reduces disease incidence. 
Maize: At land preparation time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Soybean: Before primary tillage apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Application of organic manure often corrects zinc deficiency. Apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 in a band near the row at seeding.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and calcium are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter should be improved.  To raise soil pH and calcium content liming with 200 kg/acre of agricultural lime is recommended at least 3 weeks before planting. 
Maize/ 
Beans: During the land preparation apply 3 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 100 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 3 tons/acre of manure or compost mixed with 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN.

Both fields have similar soil fertility condition. The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter should be improved. 
Tomato/Capsicum/Cabbage: Prior to transplanting apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 20-25 cm high. Top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN after 3-4 weeks. 
Melons: At planting time apply up to 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and mix well with the soil. At transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. After thinning the crop is top dressed with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Onion/ Beans: During the land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting time apply along the drill line 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17.

Maize:  At planting apply 1 handful of well rotten manure (5t/ha) mixed well with soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100kg/ha CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and at flowering. Beans:   Apply 5t/ha of well decomposed manure along the furrows mixed well with soil. Plant rhizobium innoculated seeds. Avoid acidic fertilizers such as urea, sulphate of ammonia and DAP.

The soil pH is acidic for the growth of these crops. Soil fertility status for both fields is similar. Nitrogen and zinc are low while organic matter content is moderate. 
Tomatoes:  At transplanting apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure/compost mixed well with soil per planting hole. Top dress with 1 teaspoonful of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when the crop is well established. At flowering top dress with 2 teaspoonfuls of CAN. To supply zinc apply foliar fertilizer containing this nutrient once every 2 weeks prior to flowering. Cabbage:  At transplanting apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure/compost mixed well with soil per planting hole. Top dress with 1 teaspoonful of CAN when the crop is well established after transplanting and later with another 1 teaspoonful CAN When the heads start to form. Sukuma:  At transplanting apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure/compost mixed well with soil per planting hole. Top dress with 1 teaspoonful of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when the crop is well established and thereafter apply 1 teaspoonful of CAN after every 3 weeks.

Sukamawiki:  At transplanting apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure/compost mixed well with soil per planting hole. The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Capsicum: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. When plants are about 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) and later about 4 weeks with 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Cinnamon: At land preparation time apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain the adequate level of organic matter. At planting apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP) or 50 kg/acre of triple superphosphate (TSP). At the commencement of the rains top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Fertilizer is to be applied at 50 cm radius around the plant or between rows. After application of fertilizer it is important to fork it into soil. 
Garlic: At planting time apply along the drill line 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 100 kg/acre of SSP or 50 kg/acre of TSP. One month after planting top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). At the start of flowering top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of SSP. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 80 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Spinach: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of SSP. Mix well with the soil. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3 weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Capsicum/ Chilli: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP) or 50 kg/acre of triple superphosphate (TSP). When plants are about 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) and later about 4 weeks with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of SSP or 50 kg/acre of TSP. At the start of flowering top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Maize: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN. 
Beans: Several days before planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Plant rhizobium inoculated seeds.

Both fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Soil fertility is poor. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Beans: To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax one month before planting. Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Melons/ 
Cucumber: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP) or 60 kg/acre of triple superphosphate (TSP). After thinning top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Garlic: At planting time apply along the drill line 120 kg/acre of SSP or 60 kg/acre of TSP. One month after planting top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Magnesium is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH and magnesium content apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Maize: At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Banana: At planting apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost and 50 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 mixed well with top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm per stool of CAN in 2 split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. 
Beetroot: At least 7 days before planting apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with soil.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Soil organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Kale/ 
Spinach: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with soil.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Turnip: Apply 50 kg/acre of CAN a few days before sowing. 
Strawberry: At planting apply 50 kg/acre of CAN. At flowering time apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. Deflower to control premature cropping until the crop is well established.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. 
Grapes: The preferred soil pH for a new crop is 6.5. At transplanting time apply 50 gm of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17, 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost and mix well with 2 handfuls of top soil per planting hole. Fill the hole with water and wait till the water is soaked up into the ground. Lower the plant carefully into the hole and fill the hole with soil and irrigate the crop immediately and there after at regular intervals. 3-4 months after transplanting top dress with 50 gm/plant of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Apple/ Avocado: At transplanting time apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter is sufficient. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Fruit trees: At transplanting time apply 1 handful of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of single superphosphate (SSP) or 50 gm of triple superphosphate (TSP) and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
French beans: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate(CAN) at 2-3 leave stage and 100 kg/acre at the onset of flowering. 
Melons: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. After thinning the crop should be top dressed with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Egg plant: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dressing should be done once per month for four months with 20 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Celery: At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Apply fortnightly dressings of CAN at 40 kg/acre beginning after transplanting. 
Courgettes: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. After thinning the crop should be top dressed with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Chilli: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP) or 40 kg/acre of triple superphosphate (TSP). At the start of flowering apply 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Coriander: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of SSP or 40 kg/acre of TSP. Top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN six weeks later. 
Carrot: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of SSP or 40 kg/acre of TSP. When the crop is 12 cm high apply 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter is sufficient. At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. 
Cucumber: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP) or 50 kg/acre of triple superphosphate (TSP). After thinning the crop should be top dressed with 50 kg/acre of CAN followed by 100 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of SSP or 50 kg/acre of TSP. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 100 kg/acre of SSP or 50 kg/acre of TSP. One month after planting top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter should be improved. To raise soil pH apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Parsley: Before planting the parsley apply in the bed single compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. After the first cutting work into the soil 6 inches from the plant and water thoroughly afterward using 50 kg/acre of CAN and an additional 50 kg/acre following each subsequent cutting. This process is called side-dressing the plants. The water dilutes the fertilizer so that it doesn't cause burning by the time it reaches the plant. Rinsing the leaves of the parsley after each fertilizer application to ensure that no fertilizer remains on the foliage to cause burning. 
Leeks: Before transplanting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later. 
Peas: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At flowering stage top dress with CAN at the rate of 50 kg/acre.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter should be improved. 
Eryngium: This plant thrives in dry, sandy, poor soils. It’s not necessary to fertilize eryngium plant. During land preparation 2 tons/acre of well decompose manure or compost and 80 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 might be applied.

At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

French beans: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) at 2-3 leave stage and 100 kg/acre at the onset of flowering.

The soil pH satisfactory for 
Beans' growth. Acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. Recommendations: Apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 along the ridges before planting. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

The soil pH is alkaline for 
Tea growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. Recommendations: Tea grows best on fertile soils with pH of 4.0-6.0. Broadcast on the ground 50 gm per bush of diammonium phosphate (DAP). Manure should be applied at the rate of 5 ton/ha per year.

Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 100 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Vegetables: At transplanting time apply 100 kg/acre of SSP. Mix well with the soil. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 40 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Soil fertility is poor. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise the soil pH, calcium and magnesium content liming with 300 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax is recommended 4 weeks before planting. 
Maize: At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Soil fertility is poor. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise the soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax 4 weeks before planting. 
Potato: At planting time apply in the ridge 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. The manure and fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with potato seeds. 
Macadamia: At transplanting time apply a bucket of 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Apply 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Banana: At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm of CAN per stool in year one and thereafter 100 gm of CAN per year at the beginning of rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for trees' growth. Nitrogen and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Banana: At the beginning of rains apply 50 gm per tree of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. Manure or compost should be applied 1 bucket of 10 kg per tree per year. All nutrient materials should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Coffee: Coffee grows best on fertile soils with pH of 5.3-6.0. An application of well decomposed manure or compost is recommended at the rate of 10 kg per tree per year. Also apply 50 gm/tree of DAP in 3-4 equal applications per year. All nutrient material should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Macadamia: At transplanting time apply a bucket of 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with a teaspoonful of zinc sulphate, 100 gm of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied one or two weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Chilli: At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Potassium is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Strawberry: To raise the soil pH apply 250 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 140 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. At flowering time top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Mulching is recommended to reduce weeds, conserve moisture and keep the fruits clean.

Both fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise the soil pH and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Melons: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. After thinning the crop should be top dressed with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Sukuma wiki: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks.

The soil reaction (pH) is very acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 800 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Sunflower: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 two inches below the seed bed. Don't place the fertilizer with the sunflower seeds. Top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) at 4 and 8 weeks after germination. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Maize: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. 
Capsicum: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Tomato: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of SSP. At the start of flowering apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Maize: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of SSP. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

Maize: During the land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Wheat: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 2 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. Rotation of 3 years wheat followed by 3 years of grass helps to maintain the soil structure and greatly reduces disease incidence.

Fruit trees: At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with a teaspoon of zinc sulphate, 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Horticultural crops: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later. 
Banana: At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with a teaspoon of zinc sulphate, 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm of CAN per stool in year one and thereafter 100 gm of CAN per year at the beginning of rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Green corn: At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) at 100 kg/acre in 2 equal split applications at knee height after 1 month and when plants are about to flower. 
Soya bean: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. 
Trees: At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Tomato: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Wheat: Wheat prefers neutral to slightly acidic soil. At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply by spreading 140 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). 
French beans: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 140 kg/acre of SSP. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN at 2-3 leave stage and 100 kg/acre at the onset of flowering. 
Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 gm of SSP per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 100 gm/plant of CAN. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for trees' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Fruit trees: At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc before flowering. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. 
Capsicum (Pepper): At planting apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Melons: At planting apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. After thinning top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN followed by 100 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, magnesium and organic matter are low. 
Onions: Apply 6 tons/acre of manure or compost and 160 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 as basic dressing. Top dress with 120 kg/acre of CAN when plants have started growing well after transplanting. 
Kales: At transplanting apply  6 tons/acre of manure or compost and 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 20-25 cm high followed by80 kg/acre of CAN four weeks later. 
Tomatoes: Prior to transplanting apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial.

All fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Maize: At land preparation apply 1-2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 140 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). When maize is about a knee high top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

Both fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. 
Maize: At land preparation apply 4 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 4 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Avocado: At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN. The fertilizer should be used in two split applications during the year at the beginning of rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2-4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Maize: At planting time apply 150 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Vegetables: At planting time apply 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20-25 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Phosphorus and calcium are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 250 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil pH is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, magnesium and organic matter are low. 
Onions: Apply 6 tons/acre of manure or compost and 160 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 as basic dressing. Top dress with 120 kg/acre of CAN when plants have started growing well after transplanting. 
Kales: At transplanting apply  6 tons/acre of manure or compost and 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 20-25 cm high followed by80 kg/acre of CAN four weeks later. 
Tomatoes: Prior to transplanting apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 100 kg/acre of CAN at the start of flowering is beneficial.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen and magnesium are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Chilli: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At transplanting apply 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Sweet potato: Just before planting apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. The manure should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 4 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for trees' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 5.5-6.5. Apply 10 kg per tree of well decomposed manure or compost. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0 in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. All nutrient materials should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Brassicas: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, potassium, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Maize: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen and magnesium are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolomite (dolmax or magmax) 3-4 weeks before planting. 
Chilli: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At transplanting apply 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Sweet potato: Just before planting apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. The manure should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 4 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Spinach: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is very acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium, magnesium and zinc are deficient. Potassium is at deficiency threshold level. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply 500 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Spinach: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium and calcium are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise the soil pH and calcium  content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 5.5-6.5. At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 150 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Garden peas: At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. Several days before sowing apply 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. At flowering stage top dress with CAN at the rate of 50 kg/acre. 
Tomato: At land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting apply 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17.

The soil reaction (pH) is very acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus and calcium are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 800 kg/acre of agricultural lime 4 weeks before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil.

Sunflower: At planting apply 200 kg/ha of N:P:K 123:23:0  fertilizer. Top dress with 100 kg/ha of CAN fertilizer when the crop is 20 cm.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Tomato: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen and calcium are deficient. Soil zinc and organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and calcium content apply through broadcasting 500 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. 
Potato: At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. Just before planting apply 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. The fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise soil pH and calcium content apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Capsicum: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Kale: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for trees' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 6.2-6.5. Apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost, 200 gm of agricultural lime and 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer for each tree per year on the onset of each long and short rains. All nutrient materials should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Macadamia: Macadamia nut trees can be grown on well-drained soils with a pH of 5.0-6.5. Apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 200 gm of agricultural lime and 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 per tree in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil.

The top soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Coffee: Coffee grows best on fertile, loam soils with pH of 5.3-6.0. At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with a teaspoon of zinc sulphate, 150 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Apply 100 gm/tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in 3-4 equal applications per year. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Maize: At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

Citrus trees: Apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer per tree. At planting time apply along the drill line 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 or 19:19:19 fertilizer. Mix well with the soil. One month after planting top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost, 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 or 19:19:19. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium, magnesium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply through broadcasting 250 kg/acre of dolomitic lime one month before planting. 
Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 100 gm/plant of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To correct zinc deficiency apply a foliar feed containing zinc in accordance with manufacture's instructions/ guide. 
Sweet potato: Just before planting apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. The fertilizer should be well worked into the soil.

Capsicum (Pepper): When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of ASN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of ASN.

Hay: At planting apply 140 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for plants' growth. Phosphorus is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Flowers: During the bed preparation incorporate in the soil 5 kg/m2 of well decomposed manure or compost and mix well with the soil. At planting apply 100 gm/m2 of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. Top dress at an interval of two months with 50 gm/m2 of CAN. 
Pumpkin: At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. After thinning top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN followed by 120 kg/acre of CAN prior to flowering.
