Both blocks have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Avocado: At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 100 gm/plant of CAN. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. To correct zinc deficiency apply a foliar feed containing zinc in accordance with manufacture's instructions/ guide. 
Tomato: Tomatoes need well-drained soil with lots of organic matter. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2-4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of  N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Apply foliar fertilizer (Bayfolan) with crop protection sprays or at 7-10 day intervals. Begin 3 weeks after emergence.

Cabbage: At planting time apply 60 kg/acre of CAN. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Sukuma wiki: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of CAN. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks.

Tomato: At planting time apply 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is very acidic for crops' growth. Phosphorus, calcium, magnesium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Cabbage: To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply through broadcasting 800 kg/acre of dolomitic lime one month before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Calcium is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Tomato: To raise soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime. At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter. Mix well with the soil. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for trees' growth. Nitrogen, calcium and magnesium are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. 
Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 5.5-6.5. At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 200 gm of dolomitic lime, 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in two equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of each long and short rains. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Copper is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. At land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost in order to maintain adequate level of organic matter and 4 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Maize: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of single superphosphate (SSP). Top dress with 80 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering. 
Potato: Just before planting apply 80 kg/acre of SSP. The fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of SSP. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN.

Tomato: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 50 kg/acre of CAN. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Kale: At planting time apply 50 kg/acre of CAN. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and calcium content apply 250 kg/acre of agricultural lime. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Potato: Just before planting apply 60 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. The fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the drill line 60 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Ginger: At planting time apply 60 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 120 kg/acre of CAN in two split doses at the 45 and 90 days after planting.

The top soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen is deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Tomato: At planting apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 20:10:10 fertilizer. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 50 kg/acre of N:P:K 20:10:10. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN.

All fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' cultivation. Nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Beans: To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost, 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Onion: To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. At planting time apply along the drill line 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost, 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. One month after planting top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Banana: They prefer soil pH between 5.5 and 6.5. At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of agricultural lime, 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm of CAN per stool in year one and thereafter 100 gm/stool of CAN per year at the beginning of rains. To correct zinc deficiency spray growing trees with a foliar feed.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Hence, acidifying fertilizer like DAP should be avoided. Nitrogen and magnesium are deficient. Soil phosphorus and organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and magnesium content liming with 300 kg/acre of dolomite is recommended at least 3 weeks before planting. 
Tomato: Prior to transplanting apply 4 kg/m2 of well decomposed manure or compost. When transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 40 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) when plants are 25 cm high. An application of 80 kg/acre of CAN 4 weeks later is beneficial. 
Capsicum: During the land preparation apply 5 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At transplanting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 40 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 80 kg/acre of CAN. 
Onion: Apply up to 5 tons/acre of manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 as basic dressing. Top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN when plants have started growing well after transplanting.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and calcium are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. 
Rhodes grass: Apply 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. 
Maize: At land preparation apply 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus, magnesium and copper are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. To raise soil pH and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolomitic lime. At land preparation apply 4 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Tomato: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. 
Kale: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 60 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for herbs' growth. Nitrogen, potassium, calcium, magnesium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. 
Basil/ 
Thyme/ 
Mint: To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply through broadcasting 500 kg/acre of dolomitic lime one month before planting. At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 or 19:19:19 fertilizer. Top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) six weeks after planting.

Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost, 200 gm of agricultural lime and 50 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 100 gm/plant of CAN . The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Pomegranate: Trees grow best in a soil pH range of 5.5 to 7.2. At transplanting time apply 20 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 200 gm of agricultural lime, 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 and top soil per planting hole. Transplanting should be done at the beginning of rains. Top dress 100 gm per tree of CAN in two equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is slightly alkaline for crops' growth. Copper is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Maize: To maintain adequate level of organic matter at land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of urea in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering. 
Beans: Before sowing apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Peas: Several days before sowing apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of copper sulphate. At flowering stage top dress with urea at the rate of 25 kg/acre.

Beans: Before sowing apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

Potato: Just before planting apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 120 of SSP (or 60 kg/acre of TSP). The manure and fertilizer should be well worked into the soil to avoid close contact with the seeds. 
Fruit trees: At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of SSP (or 50 gm of TSP) and top soil per planting hole. Top dress 100 gm per tree of CAN in two equal split applications 1-2 weeks after of each long and short rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for trees' growth. Zinc is deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. 
Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 5.5-6.5. At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) in 2 equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of each long and short rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Macadamia: Macadamia nut trees can be grown on well-drained soils with a pH of 5.0-6.5. At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 50 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in 2 equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Sorghum: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Three weeks after sowing top dress with 60 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN). 
Beans: Before sowing apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Okra: At planting time apply in bands on the side furrow 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with CAN in 2 equal split applications. The first top dress should be done 3-4 weeks after planting at 80 kg/acre and the second application 3-4 weeks later at the same rate.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. At land preparation apply 4 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost and 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. 
Maize: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0 fertilizer. Top dress with 80 kg/acre of CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering. 
Beans: Before sowing apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Millet: At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Three weeks after sowing top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for crops' growth. Zinc is deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation apply 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Tomato: At planting apply 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering top dress with 100 kg/acre of CAN. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Spray growing plants with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. When plants are 15 cm high top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Four weeks later apply 100 kg/acre of CAN. Spray growing plants with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Kale: At planting time apply 80 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN after every 3 weeks. Spray growing plants with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crop's cultivation. Phosphorus, potassium, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Maize: To raise soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. To maintain adequate level of organic matter at land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. To control zinc deficiency apply 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crop's cultivation. Phosphorus, potassium, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Maize: To raise soil pH and calcium content apply 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. To maintain adequate level of organic matter at land preparation apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. To control zinc deficiency apply 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17 fertilizer. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Phosphorus, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved.  To raise the soil pH and calcium content apply 250 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. 
Coffee: Coffee grows best on fertile soils with pH of 5.3-6.0. At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 and top soil per planting hole. Apply 100 gm/tree of CAN in 3-4 equal applications per year. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Macadamia: Macadamia nut trees can be grown on well-drained soils with a pH of 5.0-6.5. At transplanting time apply 10 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with 100 gm of N:P:K 23:23:0 and top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 gm per tree of CAN in 2 equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc. 
Beans: Before sowing apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost, 4 kg/acre of zinc sulphate and 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with the soil. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for grass growth. Copper is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. Grass: To maintain adequate level of organic matter apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Apply 50 kg/acre of TSP and 5 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for grass growth. Phoaphorus and copper are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. Grass: To maintain adequate level of organic matter apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Apply 50 kg/acre of TSP and 5 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for grass growth. Phoaphorus and copper are deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. Grass: To maintain adequate level of organic matter apply 1 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Apply 50 kg/acre of TSP and 5 kg/acre of copper sulphate. Mix well with the soil.

All fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen, phosphorus and calcium are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH and calcium content apply through broadcasting 300 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. 
Cabbage: At planting time apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when seedlings are established and a second top dressing at the rate of 100 kg/acre when leaves start to fold. 
Tomato: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At the start of flowering apply 100 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At early fruiting stage top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. 
Strawberry: Soil pH in the 6.0 to 7.0 range is ideal for strawberries. At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. At flowering time top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN. Mulching is recommended to reduce weeds, conserve moisture and keep the fruits clean.

All fields have similar soil fertility status. The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for 
Coffee growth. Nitrogen, potassium, calcium and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. Coffee: Coffee grows best on fertile soils with pH of 5.3-6.0. Apply 15 kg of well decomposed manure or compost, 200 gm of agricultural lime and 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 per tree in 3-4 equal applications per year. All nutrient materials should be worked lightly with a fork jembe into the soil under a tree canopy. In the fields with low zinc spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing zinc.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crop's growth. Zinc is deficient. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Maize: To raise the soil pH apply 600 kg/acre of agricultural lime one month before planting. To maintain adequate level of organic matter at land preparation apply 1 ton/acre. Also apply 5 kg/acre of zinc sulphate. Mix well with the soil. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and when plants are flowering.

The soil reaction (pH) is satisfactory for trees' growth. Soil organic matter content is sufficient. 
Avocado: The optimum soil pH is 5.5-6.5. At transplanting time apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm per tree of ammonium sulphate (AS) in 2 equal split applications 1-2 weeks after the onset of each long and short rains. 
Apple: Apple trees prefer a soil pH between 5.8 and 7.0. At transplanting time apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with top soil per planting hole. 50 gm of AS per tree should be applied 3 months after planting. This is increased at the rate of 50 gm each year to a maximum of 1 kg/tree. To determine the right amount of fertilizers the soil should be tested annually. 
Tree tomato: They grow on soils with a pH of 5 to 8.5. At transplanting time apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed well with top soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm per tree of AS. The fertilizer should be used in two equal split applications during the year at the beginning of rains.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for 
Ginger cultivation. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, manganese, copper and zinc are deficient. Soil organic matter content is low. The ginger plant grows best in loose, well drained soil with a pH of 5.5 to 6.5 high in organic matter. At land preparation apply through broadcasting 5 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. At planting time apply 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 120 kg/acre of CAN in two equal split doses at the 45 and 90 days after planting. Commercial liquid fertilizer (a foliar feed) containing manganese, copper and zinc should be used to control the deficiency.

Passion fruits: Mix top soil with 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure or compost and 100 gm of N:P:K 17:17:17 per planting hole. Fill the planting hole with this mixture using extra top soil if necessary. This should be done at least three weeks before transplanting. To stimulate the plants' growth top dress with 100 gm/plant of CAN. The fertilizer quantity should be split and applied 1-2 weeks after the onset of the rains. Spray growing plants with a foliar feed containing manganese and zinc.

Mulching is recommended to reduce weeds, conserve moisture and keep the fruits clean.

Spray growing trees with a foliar feed containing manganese and zinc.

The soil pH is satisfactory for the growth of these crops. Nitrogen, potassium, magnesium, calcium and zinc are low, while organic matter content is moderate. During land preparation apply 400 kg/ha of dolomitic lime (magmax or dolmax) and 5 kg/ha of zinc sulphate at least 4 weeks before planting. Maize: At planting apply 1 handful of well decomposed manure (5 ton/ha) mixed well with 1 teaspoonful of N:P:K 19:19:19 or 17:17:17 (200 kg/ha) and soil per planting hole. Top dress with 150 kg/ha CAN in two equal split applications at knee high and at flowering. Beans: Apply 5 ton/ha of well decomposed manure along the furrows mixed well with 50 kg/ha of N:P:K 17:17:17 and the soil. Plant rhizobium inoculated seeds. Avoid acidic fertilizers such as urea, sulphate of ammonia and DAP.

The pH of the soil is acidic for growing this crop. Magnesium and zinc are low, while organic matter content is moderate. 
Capsicum: At planting apply 5 kg of well decomposed manure or compost mixed with 150 gm of lime (Dolmax or Magmax) and 1 gm of zinc sulphate per square meter. Top dress with 60 gm of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17 per square meter when the crop is well established and another 100 gm/sq. meter of N:P:K 17:17:17 four weeks later. Apply foliar fertilizer containing zinc before flowering.

The pH of the soil is suitable for the growth of these crops. Nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, organic matter content, calcium, magnesium and zinc are low. 
Apples: At planting apply 15 kg of well decomposed manure mixed well with 150 gm of compound fertilizer N:P:K 17:17:17, 1 gm of zinc sulphate and top soil. Year 1 apply 100 kg/ha of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN), year 2 apply 250 kg/ha N:P:K 17:17:17, year 3 apply 300kg/ha N:P:K 17:17:17.  
Vegetables: Apply 2 handfuls of well decomposed manure mixed well with 1 teaspoonful of N:P:K 17:17:17 or 19:19:19  and soil per planting hole. Top dress with 100 kg/ha CAN when the crop is well established and another 100 kg/ha CAN 3-4 weeks later. 
Trees: Apply 5 kg of manure mixed well with 1 teaspoonful of N:P:K 17:17:17 and soil per planting hole. Top dress with 50 gm of CAN when the trees are well established.

The soil pH is acidic for crops' growth. Nitrogen and phosphorus are deficient. Soil organic matter content should be improved. At land preparation time apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. 
Tomato: At transplanting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) at the start of flowering. At early fruiting stage top dress with 60 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. 
Kale/ 
Spinach: At planting apply 120 kg/acre of N:P:K 23:23:0. Mix well with the soil. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN three weeks later.

The soil reaction (pH) is acidic for crops growth. Soil fertility is low. Soil organic matter content should be improved. To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply broadcasting 1 ton/acre of dolomitic lime at least one month before planting. At land preparation apply 2 ton/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. Mix well with the soil. Commercial liquid fertilizer (a foliar feed) containing manganese and zinc should be used to control the deficiency. 
Vegetables: At planting time apply 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Top dress with 50 kg/acre of CAN when plants are 20-25 cm high followed by 50 kg/acre of CAN 3-4 weeks later. 
Beans: Before planting apply by incorporation into the soil along the ridges 150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. Plant Rhizobia inoculated seeds to encourage nitrogen fixation. 
Onion: At planting time apply along the ridges150 kg/acre of N:P:K 17:17:17. One month after planting top dress with 60 kg/acre of CAN.

Maize growth in James Karanja fields. Hence acidifying fertilizers like DAP and urea should be avoided. Soil organic matter should be improved. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).

To raise soil pH, calcium and magnesium content apply 300 kg/acre of dolmax or magmax at least 3 weeks before planting. Recommendations: During the land preparation apply 2 tons/acre of well decomposed manure or compost. At planting time apply 100 kg/acre of compound fertilizer N:P:K 23:23:0. When maize is about a knee high, top dress with 50 kg/acre of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN).
